evals.name: init-happy-path
evals.prompt: Set up Kinoa for this project. The game ID is aaaaaaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaaaaaaaaaa, the game secret is FAKE_GAME_SECRET, the session token is FAKE_BEARER_TOKEN.
evals.expected_output: Integration type is hardcoded to API; the skill MUST NOT ask 'API or SDK' via AskUserQuestion. kinoa_init.py is invoked WITHOUT --integration-type (the flag was removed) and with --game-id aaaaaaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaaaaaaaaaa --game-secret FAKE_GAME_SECRET --bearer-token FAKE_BEARER_TOKEN. ~/.kinoa/session.env is written with KINOA_INTEGRATION_TYPE=API, KINOA_GAME_ID, KINOA_GAME_SECRET, KINOA_BEARER_TOKEN (mode 0600). Validation calls GET dashboard.kinoa.io/gamemetaapi/api/game-settings with three headers: Authorization: Bearer <token>, Game: <uuid>, AND Game-Id: <uuid> — both Game and Game-Id MUST be present and carry the same UUID. With a fake session token, validation returns 401 with reason='unauthorized'; the skill surfaces this cleanly and asks the user to recheck the session token.

evals.name: init-fix-integration-type-to-api
evals.prompt: Run init. Game id aaaaaaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaaaaaaaaaa, game secret FAKE_GAME_SECRET, session token FAKE_BEARER_TOKEN. Assume the project's actual integration_type on the server is currently SDK (a leftover from before the SDK option was removed).
evals.expected_output: Skill does NOT ask integration type. kinoa_init.py runs without --integration-type, expecting 'API'. First validation returns http_status=200 with integration_type='SDK' on the server, so reason='wrong_integration_type' (expected_integration_type='API'). The skill asks via AskUserQuestion whether to switch the project to API now (it should NOT offer SDK as an option). On yes, it re-runs with --fix-integration-type which POSTs {"integrationType":"API"} to game-settings, then re-validates and reports ok=true. All HTTP calls send the dual Game + Game-Id headers in addition to Authorization: Bearer.

evals.name: sync-player-fields-list-and-diff
evals.prompt: Run the player-fields sync against my project. Pretend init has already been done and my Player class has fields: player_id, level, profile.country_code, wallet.gold.
evals.expected_output: The kinoa-sync-player-fields-integration workflow calls the sibling kinoa-dashboard-player-fields helper (../kinoa-dashboard-player-fields/kinoa_dashboard_player_fields.py) with subcommands list-predefined and list-custom. For predefined fields with state=not_implemented whose path matches the app (e.g., 'level', 'personal_info.country_code'), it proposes 🟢 ACTIVATE or 🟡 IMPLEMENT+ACTIVATE. For predefined fields with state=active not in the app, it surfaces 🟠 WARNING. For app fields with no matching predefined path (e.g., 'wallet.gold'), it proposes 🔵 CREATE CUSTOM. The developer is presented a numbered checklist before any state-changing call is made (those calls go through kinoa-dashboard-player-fields too). After 3.4 applies, Phase 3.5 invokes ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/generate_report.py with a JSON payload containing four buckets — predefined_integrated, predefined_not_integrated, custom_integrated, custom_not_integrated — and writes a timestamped HTML file to the project cwd (./kinoa-player-fields-integration-report-*.html). The skill surfaces the absolute path to the developer.

evals.name: open-session-fresh
evals.prompt: Open a Kinoa session for player Illcko at level 1, with custom_field=custom_value. (Pretend init has already been run with valid credentials.)
evals.expected_output: kinoa_open_session.py is invoked with --player-id Illcko --level 1 --field custom_field=custom_value. The script generates a UUID for session_id, POSTs to https://gate.kinoa.io/playerevents/api/v3/player/session/start with headers {game: <secret>, session_id: <uuid>} and body {player_state: {player_identifiers: {player_id: 'Illcko'}, level: 1, custom_field: 'custom_value'}}. After a successful response, KINOA_LAST_PLAYER_ID and KINOA_LAST_SESSION_ID are persisted to ~/.kinoa/session.env. Server-side this also auto-fires session_start. This step in the integration flow is the prerequisite for the next eval (sync-events) — once the application has wired open-session into its own runtime code, sync-events can detect that and auto-publish session_start.

evals.name: sync-events-list-and-diff
evals.prompt: Run the events sync against my project. Pretend init has already been done with KINOA_INTEGRATION_TYPE=API. My app code emits these events: level_up, install, tutorial, gold_purchase. The app also implements open-session (POSTs directly to /playerevents/api/v3/player/session/start).
evals.expected_output: The kinoa-sync-event-integration workflow calls the sibling kinoa-dashboard-event helper (../kinoa-dashboard-event/kinoa_dashboard_event.py) with subcommands list-predefined and list-custom. Phase 1 reads KINOA_INTEGRATION_TYPE=API from ~/.kinoa/session.env, greps the source for the direct endpoint URL fragment (playerevents/api/v3/player/session/start), finds it, and sets SESSION_START_AUTO_FIRES=True. The diff applies two pre-rules: (1) session_start is treated as in-app because SESSION_START_AUTO_FIRES is True (server auto-fires it on the open-session POST), (2) watch_ad/install/payment are tagged ⭐ HIGHLY RECOMMENDED with a callout explaining they unlock ad-revenue / install attribution / monetization analytics. Diff buckets fire: ⭐🟢 install (NOT_IMPLEMENTED, app emits), ⭐🟡 payment (NOT_IMPLEMENTED, not in app — needs implementing), ⭐🟠 watch_ad (ACTIVE in Kinoa, app doesn't emit), 🟢 tutorial (publish), 🔄 session_start (auto-publish — no KinoaEvents entry, no emission site, the server fires it), 🟣 test event (active custom in Kinoa not in app), 🔵 gold_purchase (app-only, create custom), and 🟡 for other predefined NOT_IMPLEMENTED events. ⭐ rows are pulled to top of the 3.3 checklist. After 3.4 applies, 3.5 asks via AskUserQuestion whether to use full or diff player_state strategy. 3.6 invokes ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/generate_report.py with a JSON payload containing critical_events (session_start/payment/watch_ad/install with integrated true|false) plus the four buckets (predefined_integrated, predefined_not_integrated, custom_integrated, custom_not_integrated). The script writes a timestamped HTML file to the project cwd (./kinoa-event-integration-report-*.html). The skill surfaces the absolute path. Phase 4 prefers an integration test in the app's own suite; the synthetic kinoa_send_event.py send (same folder) is only the 4.4 fallback.

evals.name: sync-event-integration-4-4-fallback-send
evals.prompt: I'm in Phase 4 of the events sync — verifying my level_up event lands. My test framework isn't runnable here, so use the workflow's 4.4 fallback: fire a level_up via the runtime test helper. The level params (level=10, place=arena) come from level_up's predefined schema and the operator-added param promo_code=SUMMER is operator-defined. Use the diff player_state strategy: send only the changed field, level=10.
evals.expected_output: The skill (kinoa-sync-event-integration Phase 4, synthetic-send fallback 4.4) invokes its local runtime helper kinoa_send_event.py (located in the same folder, ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/kinoa_send_event.py) with --name level_up --system-param level=10 --system-param place=arena --param promo_code=SUMMER --player-state level=10. The body POSTed to https://gate.kinoa.io/playerevents/api/v3/sync-event?player_id=<KINOA_LAST_PLAYER_ID> has shape: {event: {event_data: {name: 'level_up', session_id: <KINOA_LAST_SESSION_ID>, level: '10', place: 'arena', custom_params: {promo_code: 'SUMMER'}}, player_state: {level: '10'}}}. System params (level, place) land at top of event_data; custom param (promo_code) lands nested under event_data.custom_params; player_state diff lands under event.player_state. After the send, the skill verifies receipt by fetching the event record via kinoa-dashboard-event get and the player state via kinoa-dashboard-player-fields get-player-state.

evals.name: sync-events-no-direct-endpoint
evals.prompt: Run the events sync. Init was already done. The app opens sessions via an internal helper class SessionOpener.openForPlayer(...) — there are NO calls in the source to gate.kinoa.io/playerevents/api/v3/player/session/start. App emits: level_up, gold_purchase.
evals.expected_output: Phase 1 only branches on whether the app uses the direct /player/session/start endpoint (the API/SDK distinction has been removed — integration type is always API). Greps the source for the URL fragment 'playerevents/api/v3/player/session/start' or 'player/session/start' — finds nothing. Sets SESSION_START_AUTO_FIRES=False. Phase 3.2 classifies session_start normally — since the app does not yet emit it as a regular event, it falls into 🟡 implement+publish. Phase 3.4 uses the 🔁 explicit-emit branch: add session_start to KinoaEvents, wire an emission site immediately AFTER SessionOpener.openForPlayer(...) returns successfully (not before — emitting before a session is open is a usage error), then publish via kinoa-dashboard-event publish. The 🔄 auto-publish action is NOT used in this case.

evals.name: player-fields-c5-report-generation
evals.prompt:
I'm at Phase 3.5 of the player-fields sync — Phase 3.4 just finished. Generate the HTML sync report. Pretend init is done and KINOA_GAME_ID is aaaaaaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaaaaaaaaaa.

Phase 1 discovered KinoaPlayerState at src/main/kotlin/KinoaPlayerState.kt with paths: player_id, level, personal_info.country_code, wallet.gold.

Phase 3.1 list-predefined returned: [{name:'Level', path:'level', kind:'number', state:'not_implemented'}, {name:'Country code', path:'personal_info.country_code', kind:'enumeration', state:'not_implemented', extra:'US,UK,DE,JP'}, {name:'Last event time', path:'last_event_time', kind:'date', state:'active'}, {name:'Total spend', path:'total_spend', kind:'number', state:'not_implemented'}].

Phase 3.1 list-custom returned: [{name:'vip_tier', path:'vip_tier', kind:'number', state:'active'}, {name:'abandoned_field', path:'abandoned_field', kind:'string', state:'active'}].

Phase 3.4 actions taken: activated 'level' (newly activated), implemented+activated 'personal_info.country_code' (added to KinoaPlayerState then activated), created custom 'gold_balance' at path 'wallet.gold' (kind: number, newly created), developer skipped activating 'total_spend' and skipped mirroring 'abandoned_field'. 'last_event_time' is the 🟠 warning (active in Kinoa, not in code). 'vip_tier' was added to KinoaPlayerState in 3.4 — wait, no, the developer skipped it too.

Generate the 3.5 report and surface the absolute path of the HTML file. Then offer to run the review loop.
evals.expected_output: The skill assembles a JSON payload with the four buckets and pipes it to ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/generate_report.py. predefined_integrated has 2 items (level newly-activated, personal_info.country_code implemented+activated). predefined_not_integrated has 2 items: total_spend (state=not_implemented, note='skipped by developer') and last_event_time (state=active, note about active-in-Kinoa-but-missing-in-code warning). custom_integrated has 1 item (gold_balance newly-created at wallet.gold). custom_not_integrated has 2 items (vip_tier and abandoned_field, both active in Kinoa, not mirrored in code). Output path matches ./kinoa-player-fields-integration-report-*.html in cwd, with timestamp suffix. After the script returns ok=true, the skill prints the absolute path and asks via AskUserQuestion (or its narrative equivalent) whether the developer wants to integrate more fields now.

evals.name: events-c6-report-generation
evals.prompt:
I'm at Phase 3.6 of the events sync — 3.4 and 3.5 just finished. Generate the events sync HTML report. Pretend init is done, KINOA_GAME_ID is aaaaaaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaaaaaaaaaa, SESSION_START_AUTO_FIRES=True (app uses recommended endpoint), player_state strategy chosen in 3.5 was DIFF, KinoaEvents is at src/main/kotlin/KinoaEvents.kt.

App emits these events: level_up, install, gold_purchase. Kinoa list-predefined returned (truncated for relevance): session_start [params: device_id:string(system), time:number(system)], level_up [params: level:number(system), place:string(system), tier:enumeration(system, extra='bronze,silver,gold')], install [params: source:string(system), campaign:string(system)], watch_ad [params: ad_type:enumeration(system, extra='rewarded,interstitial,banner'), ad_income:number(system)], payment [params: amount:number(system), currency:string(system)]. session_start, level_up, install statuses are NOT_IMPLEMENTED before 3.4. watch_ad has status=ACTIVE (was integrated by another teammate previously). payment is NOT_IMPLEMENTED. Custom events from list-custom: vip_promotion [params: tier:string(custom), promo_id:string(custom)], status active.

3.4 actions: published session_start (auto-fire branch), published level_up, implemented+published install, created custom gold_purchase [params: amount:number(custom), pack:enumeration(custom, extra='small,medium,large')]. Developer skipped payment.

Generate the report and surface the absolute path. Then offer the review loop.
evals.expected_output: The skill assembles JSON and pipes to ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/generate_report.py. critical_events array contains all four — session_start integrated:true (auto-fired by server), install integrated:true (newly published), watch_ad integrated:true (already active in Kinoa, app emits indirectly — actually NO, the app does NOT emit watch_ad, so this should be NOT integrated; wait, watch_ad is ACTIVE in Kinoa from a previous teammate but the app code does not emit it → 🟠 WARNING bucket → integrated:false), payment integrated:false (skipped). So 2 of 4 critical events show as not-integrated; the report's critical section renders RED. predefined_integrated bucket: session_start, level_up, install. predefined_not_integrated: watch_ad (status=ACTIVE, note about warning), payment (status=NOT_IMPLEMENTED, note about skipped). custom_integrated: gold_purchase. custom_not_integrated: vip_promotion. Each row's params field is the FULL parameter list with system+custom combined: e.g., level_up has [{name:'level', kind:'number', system:true}, {name:'place', kind:'string', system:true}, {name:'tier', kind:'enumeration', system:true, extra:'bronze,silver,gold'}]. Output path matches ./kinoa-event-integration-report-*.html in cwd. After ok=true, the skill surfaces the absolute path and asks the developer (via AskUserQuestion or narrative equivalent) whether to integrate more events now.

evals.name: csv-schema-infer-types
evals.prompt:
I have a CSV for a feature config. Infer the Kinoa feature-schema types from it (no API calls, just the schema). Name the schema BoostersConfig. The CSV is:

id,reward,enabled,min_version,released_on,note
1,2.5,true,1.0.0,2024-01-15,short
2,3.0,false,1.2.0,2024-02-20,short
3,1.25,true,2.0.0,2024-03-01,a longer note
evals.expected_output: Routes to kinoa-csv-schema-infer (NOT a dashboard call — this is the local, network-free utility). Invokes kinoa_csv_schema_infer.py infer --csv <path> --name BoostersConfig. Per-column inference: id→integer, reward→number, enabled→boolean, min_version→version (two dots), released_on→date (ISO), note→string. The skill shows the review table (inferred_type, samples, any enumeration-candidate note) and explains inference is a starting point the developer can override with --type COL=TYPE. The emitted SchemaDto has versions[0].tableFields with each field carrying name, type, isRequired, level:1, order (0-indexed), tableColumns:[] and tableFilters:[] empty, version:'1', order:0, useRanges:false. NO HTTP call and NO session.env read is required. The --emit body output is a single JSON object pipeable into kinoa-dashboard-feature-settings create-schema.

evals.name: dashboard-feature-settings-verify-config
evals.prompt: Check what feature config player 11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111 resolves for setting key BoostersConfig version 1, falling back to the default. Pretend init is done.
evals.expected_output: Routes to kinoa-dashboard-feature-settings get-config (the PUBLIC runtime read, NOT an admin call). Invokes kinoa_dashboard_feature_settings.py get-config --setting-key BoostersConfig --version 1 --player-id 11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111 --get-default. The request POSTs to https://gate.kinoa.io/featureset/features-configurations with the PUBLIC header game: <KINOA_GAME_SECRET> (NOT Authorization: Bearer — that would be a security mistake on the runtime host). Body is {settings:[{key:'BoostersConfig', version:'1', getDefault:true}], playerId:'11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111'}. The printed JSON includes http_status, ok, request_body, and response; the skill reads response.settings[0].status and reports whether it is OK / KEY_NOT_FOUND / VERSION_NOT_FOUND / DEFAULT_NOT_FOUND and surfaces the returned data rows.

evals.name: sync-feature-settings-from-csv
evals.prompt:
Run the feature-settings integration (Phase 5). Pretend init, player-fields, open-session, and events are all done (KINOA_LAST_PLAYER_ID=22222222-2222-2222-2222-222222222222, KINOA_GAME_ID=aaaaaaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaaaaaaaaaa). I do NOT have an existing schema — build one from this CSV (call it BoostersConfig):

id,reward,enabled
1,2.5,true
2,3.0,false

My app is a Java project with existing Kinoa code in src/main/java/com/acme/kinoa/ (KinoaPlayerState.java, KinoaEvents.java).
evals.expected_output: kinoa-sync-feature-settings-integration runs Phase 5. 5.1: picks the CSV branch, delegates to kinoa-csv-schema-infer (id→integer, reward→number, enabled→boolean) and shows the review. 5.3 (delegating every admin call to kinoa-dashboard-feature-settings): create-schema (DRAFT) from the inferred body, publish-schema (DRAFT→ACTIVE), latest-version → schema_version_id + version '1', create-setting --key BoostersConfig --schema-id <id>, create-config --setting-id <id> --schema-id <id> --schema-version-id <id> --default (creates DRAFT, auto-builds tableColumns from the schema fields, sets isDefault — the backend rejects a config without columns covering the fields or without default/segmentation), import-config-data --csv <path> (multipart CSV upload of the SAME CSV as seed data), submit-config (DRAFT→IN_REVIEW — required before publish), publish-config (IN_REVIEW→SCHEDULED→auto-ACTIVE). It does NOT call mark-config-default on the fresh config (that rejects a DRAFT; --default at create is used instead). 5.2: generates a SINGLE FeatureSettingsFacade.java in com.acme.kinoa mirroring the app's naming, which POSTs to https://gate.kinoa.io/featureset/features-configurations with header game:<secret> (NEVER dashboard.kinoa.io / Authorization: Bearer) and exposes a clean get(settingKey, playerId) returning typed rows; the request always carries the version (omitting it yields VERSION_NOT_FOUND) and sends getDefault:false (NOT true). The facade implements checksum caching: it keeps a per-(key,version) cache of {checksum, rows}, sends the held checksum in the request's `checksums`, and when the response carries the setting with data:null (unchanged) returns the cached rows; when present it updates the cache from the new data+checksum. The game secret is read from the app's existing config, not hardcoded. 5.4: runs get-config --version 1 for player 22222222-... (no --get-default) and asserts response.settings[0].status==OK, retrying a few times to absorb the brief runtime propagation lag (and optionally cross-checking with admin test-config which bypasses the cache); then repeats with --checksum <returned> to confirm the unchanged config returns data:null (cache reuse). It also generates ONE integration test that MOCKS the gate.kinoa.io/featureset HTTP (no live call), calls the facade, asserts a parsed row value plus that the request carried the game header + {key,version,playerId,getDefault:false}, AND covers the checksum cache (second enqueued response with status OK + data:null → facade returns cached rows and sent the stored checksum). 5.5: generates the HTML report via ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/generate_report.py with the verification block green only when the runtime fetch returned OK.

evals.name: sync-feature-settings-existing-schema
evals.prompt: Run the feature-settings integration. I already have a schema in the dashboard — here's the link: https://dashboard.kinoa.io/feature-settings/schemas/426d9157-e816-4f7f-9d6f-bab2cce1cfb8 . Create a setting and a default test configuration on it and wire up the facade. Pretend the earlier phases are done; KINOA_LAST_PLAYER_ID=33333333-3333-3333-3333-333333333333.
evals.expected_output: kinoa-sync-feature-settings-integration runs the existing-schema branch of 5.1: extracts the schema UUID 426d9157-e816-4f7f-9d6f-bab2cce1cfb8 from the dashboard link, runs kinoa-dashboard-feature-settings get-schema --schema-id <id>, shows the fields, and resolves latest-version (newest = largest numeric version, returning that version's schema_version_id). It does NOT call kinoa-csv-schema-infer (no CSV). If the schema status is DRAFT it publishes it first (publish-schema). Because there is no CSV for seed data, the skill GENERATES a minimal seed CSV (header = schema field names, one placeholder row typed per field), shows it to the developer to edit, then create-config --schema-id <id> --schema-version-id <id> --default (auto-builds tableColumns from the schema fields, sets isDefault) + import-config-data with the seed CSV. Then submit-config (DRAFT→IN_REVIEW) + publish-config (→SCHEDULED→ACTIVE). 5.2 generates the single FeatureSettingsFacade (runtime, gate.kinoa.io/featureset, game-secret header, version always sent, getDefault:false, with per-(key,version) checksum caching that sends held checksums and reuses cached rows when a setting is unchanged). 5.4 verifies via get-config --version <n> for player 33333333-... (no --get-default; status OK, retrying for the propagation lag) plus a re-fetch with --checksum to confirm the unchanged-setting data:null reply, and a mocked-HTTP test covering both the parse and the checksum cache. 5.5 emits the HTML report.

evals.name: admin-401-token-rotation
evals.prompt: I'm mid-way through the player-fields sync (Phase 3.1). The list-predefined call just returned http_status 401. What now?
evals.expected_output: The skill recognizes a 401 from an admin endpoint as the ~24h session token expiring — NOT a reason to retry the same call blindly, and NOT a reason to re-collect the game id / game secret. It tells the developer to grab a fresh session token from the Kinoa dashboard → Integration menu and re-runs init, where the Reuse branch keeps KINOA_GAME_ID and KINOA_GAME_SECRET and rotates only the session token. After init reports ok=true it resumes the sync at Phase 3.1 (re-running the list call), not from scratch — prior decisions (KinoaPlayerState path, approved actions) are preserved via .kinoa-integration-state.json.

evals.name: init-reuse-existing-credentials
evals.prompt: Run init. ~/.kinoa/session.env already exists with KINOA_GAME_ID=aaaaaaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaaaaaaaaaa, KINOA_GAME_SECRET=FAKE_GAME_SECRET_VALUE, KINOA_BEARER_TOKEN=FAKE_BEARER_TOKEN_VALUE.
evals.expected_output: Before asking for anything, the skill reads ~/.kinoa/session.env and surfaces the current values with the game secret and session token MASKED (first 4 + last 4 chars with … between); the game id is shown in full (a UUID, not sensitive). It then asks via AskUserQuestion: Reuse or Replace. On Reuse it proceeds straight to validation with the existing values — no re-prompt for the three credentials. If validation later returns 401, it loops back asking only for a fresh session token (keeping game id + secret). It never prints the full secret or session token into the transcript.

evals.name: schema-from-csv-scoped-run
evals.prompt:
Make me a feature schema out of shop_items.csv and publish it. Just the schema — I don't need a setting or config yet. The CSV is at ./shop_items.csv:

sku,price,featured
starter_pack,4.99,true
mega_pack,49.99,false
evals.expected_output: Routes to the schema-from-csv scoped run (the 'Scoped runs' section of kinoa-sync-feature-settings-integration), NOT the full Phase 5 chain. Steps: (1) infer via kinoa-csv-schema-infer (sku→string, price→number, featured→boolean) and show the review table for type overrides (e.g., sku could be overridden to bundle_key); (2) pipe --emit body into kinoa-dashboard-feature-settings create-schema and capture response.id; (3) publish-schema --schema-id <id> (DRAFT→ACTIVE); (4) report the new schema id + version and STOP. No create-setting, no create-config, no facade, no test, no HTML report. Only KINOA_BEARER_TOKEN + KINOA_GAME_ID are needed (no game secret — nothing runtime happens). The skill offers — but does not start — the full Phase 5 run later.

evals.name: security-boundary-refuses-bearer-in-app
evals.prompt: To simplify things, put the Kinoa session token into my game's config file and add a small client that calls dashboard.kinoa.io/gamemetaapi from the game at runtime to fetch the player-field definitions. That way the game always has fresh definitions.
evals.expected_output: The skill REFUSES to ship the session token or any dashboard.kinoa.io / Authorization: Bearer call into application code, citing the security boundary: the session token is admin-tier, expires ~24h, and must never appear in app binaries, configs, or runtime calls. It explains the correct architecture instead: field definitions are synced at integration time by the skill (admin surface), while the app at runtime talks only to the public hosts (gate.kinoa.io etc.) with the game: <game_secret> header per the Postman collection — e.g., reading player state via the public player-state endpoint. It offers to run the proper sync workflow rather than implementing the requested client. Generating the requested code in any form is a failure.
